doc_app_layout_guide_link: Guide: See Guides / App Layout for layout patterns.
doc_avatar_a11y_spec: Prevent information duplication or confusion by properly setting the alt attribute to the username if there is an image, or aria-hidden="true" for decorative purposes.
doc_avatar_anatomy_badge: An indicator for showing login status (online/offline) or that there are unread notifications (optional).
doc_avatar_anatomy_visual: Displays a profile image, initials, or a default icon.
doc_avatar_best_practice_1: Always provide a meaningful fallback (initials or icon) for when the image fails to load.
doc_avatar_best_practice_2: Use consistent sizing across similar contexts (e.g., all avatars in a comment section should be the same size).
doc_avatar_choice_avatar_when: When you want to display specific users or brands and emphasize their individuality by linking them with names.
doc_avatar_choice_icon_when: When you want to independently represent functions or categories that do not have a specific personality (such as 'Settings' or 'Home').
doc_avatar_choice_image_when: When you want to display the content itself (such as product photos) rather than the user themselves.
doc_avatar_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on the type of information to be displayed and ease of identification.
doc_avatar_colors_desc: Specify background colors for initials or icons.
doc_avatar_content_guidelines: Layer fallbacks so that initials are displayed if there is no image, and a generic silhouette icon is displayed if initials are also unknown.
doc_avatar_design_intent: Avatar provides a visual clue for identifying users or entities at a glance. By including images or initials in addition to presenting names in text, it makes the interface more human and friendly.
doc_avatar_fallback_desc: Displays initials or icons when the image URL is invalid or missing.
doc_avatar_long_desc: A component for displaying user profile images, initials, or icons in circular or rectangular shapes. Includes built-in fallback display for missing or failed image loads.
doc_avatar_motion_spec: When functioning as a link, communicate that it is clickable by slightly increasing the size or changing the brightness on hover.
doc_avatar_scenario_chat: Chat List: Displays a combination of the other's image and online status (badge).
doc_avatar_scenario_comment: Comment List: Displays minor avatars next to contributors, intuitively making it clear whose message it is.
doc_avatar_scenario_user: User Profile: Displays at the right end of the navigation bar, opening account settings on click.
doc_avatargroup_best_practice_1: Set a reasonable max count to prevent visual clutter while conveying group size with the '+N' badge.
doc_avatargroup_best_practice_2: Use the same avatar size within a group for visual consistency.
doc_badge_best_practice_1: Keep badge text short and concise so the meaning is conveyed at a glance.
doc_badge_best_practice_2: Assign semantic meaning to colors consistently — green for success, red for error.
doc_barChart_long_desc: A chart suitable for comparing and analyzing numerical values across different categories. It is one of the most basic visualizations, allowing for quick grasping of data trends and differences.
doc_barchart_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_barchart_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_barchart_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_barchart_best_practice_1: Use BarChart consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_barchart_best_practice_2: Test BarChart behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_barchart_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Table better suits the use case.
doc_barchart_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_barchart_choice_self_when: When BarChart functionality with standard options is required.
doc_barchart_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_barchart_design_intent: BarChart provides a consistent, reusable pattern for bar chart visualization for comparing categorical data, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_barchart_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_barchart_scenario_1: Integrating BarChart into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_barchart_scenario_2: Using BarChart in management views for information organization.
doc_baseListItem_desc: Consistent list item layout for Selectbox, Menu, Dropdown, etc.
doc_baseListItem_long_desc: BaseListItem is a foundational layout component providing consistent item structure for dropdowns, menus, Selectboxes, and other list-based components.
doc_baseListItem_summary: Consistent list item layout for Selectbox, Menu, Dropdown, etc.
doc_baseListItem_usage_desc: Used internally by Selectbox, Menu, Dropdown, and Combobox to render individual list items with icon, label, and right section slots.
doc_calendar_a11y_1: Navigate through dates using keyboard arrow keys.
doc_calendar_a11y_2: Select a date using Enter or Space keys.
doc_calendar_a11y_3: Change months using Page Up and Page Down keys.
doc_calendar_a11y_4: Compliant with WAI-ARIA calendar design patterns.
doc_calendar_a11y_5: Selected and disabled states are communicated via aria-selected and aria-disabled.
doc_calendar_behavior_1: Select the start date on the first click.
doc_calendar_behavior_2: Select the end date on the second click.
doc_calendar_behavior_3: The range between start and end dates is highlighted during selection.
doc_calendar_behavior_4: If a date before the start date is selected as the end date, they are automatically swapped.
doc_calendar_controlled_uncontrolled_des: The Calendar component supports both controlled and uncontrolled patterns.
doc_calendar_disabled_by_function_desc: By passing a function to isDateDisabled, you can flexibly disable dates based on conditions like weekends or past dates.
doc_calendar_disabled_dates_desc: There are two ways to disable dates: by passing an array or a function.
doc_calendar_integration_datepicker_desc: Calendar is used as an internal component of DatePicker. Use this component directly for standalone calendar needs.
doc_calendar_long_desc: Details and usage for the Calendar component.
doc_calendar_min_max_dates_desc: Limit the selectable range with minDate and maxDate properties. Dates outside this range will be grayed out.
doc_calendar_range_mode_desc: Enable starting and ending date range selection by setting the rangeMode property.
doc_card_a11y_spec: When making the entire card a link, consider the order in which screen readers read and explore structures (such as focus on headings) to avoid redundant reading.
doc_card_anatomy_body: Displays the main content that is at the heart of the content (such as descriptions or images).
doc_card_anatomy_footer: Places action buttons such as 'View Details' or 'Buy', or accompanying information such as dates.
doc_card_anatomy_header: Places titles, meta-information, or context menus (such as meatball menus).
doc_card_anatomy_media: Places images or videos at the top or side of the card to attract visual interest (optional).
doc_card_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Card component for grouping related content.
doc_card_best_practice_1: Too many elements in a card can make info priority unclear, so focus on placing essential information.
doc_card_best_practice_2: If making the whole card clickable, set focus states (like Outline) properly to ensure accessibility.
doc_card_body_desc: The main content area of the card.
doc_card_choice_box_when: When the purpose is merely margin or placement adjustment and does not require a visual container (such as borders).
doc_card_choice_card_when: When you want to express a single independent topic (product, user profile, article summary, etc.) and distinguish it from others with borders or backgrounds.
doc_card_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on the independence of information and the degree of visual emphasis.
doc_card_choice_tile_when: When the main purpose is more grid-like and interactive selection (such as dashboard menu icons).
doc_card_content_guidelines: Limit the information included in one card to a single topic and ensure the priority of information (visual hierarchy) is clear.
doc_card_design_intent: Card is a component used to bundle related information (text, images, actions, etc.) into a single visual container. By defining 'chunks' of information, it clarifies the boundaries of each element even on complex pages, helping users understand the content quickly.
doc_card_guideline_1: Use when you want to group related information and actions in one place and maintain visual consistency.
doc_card_guideline_2: Ideal for displaying multiple independent contents, such as dashboard summaries or product listings.
doc_card_long_desc: Details and usage for the Card component.
doc_card_motion_spec: If the entire card is clickable, emphasize interactivity by adding an effect where it slightly lifts (shadow strengthens) on hover.
doc_card_scenario_product: Product List: Aggregates and arranges images, prices, ratings, and add-to-cart buttons into one card.
doc_card_scenario_profile: User Profile: Compactly displays avatar, name, role, and SNS links.
doc_card_scenario_widget: Analytics Widget: A dashboard part that combines a graph with current metrics and a link to detailed view.
doc_card_subcomponent_desc: The Card component consists of several subcomponents, each with a specific role.
doc_charts_desc: Visual representation of data with various chart types
doc_chatui_a11y_2: Send button can be triggered with Enter key
doc_chatui_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_chatui_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_chatui_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_chatui_best_practice_1: Use ChatUI consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_chatui_best_practice_2: Test ChatUI behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_chatui_bp_a11y_desc: To ensure screen reader compatibility, assign appropriate ARIA roles to the message list and notify new messages using aria-live.
doc_chatui_bp_autoscroll_desc: Implement auto-scroll to newest messages for better UX
doc_chatui_bp_avatars_desc: Display avatars for multi-user conversations to help identify senders
doc_chatui_bp_loading_desc: Show typing indicators or loading states when appropriate
doc_chatui_bp_position_desc: Use `position="left"` for received messages and `position="right"` for sent messages
doc_chatui_c_avatar: Avatar component with image support and fallback text
doc_chatui_c_input: Input field with send button for composing messages
doc_chatui_c_message: Individual message bubble with support for avatars, timestamps, and sender names
doc_chatui_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Dialog better suits the use case.
doc_chatui_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_chatui_choice_self_when: When ChatUI functionality with standard options is required.
doc_chatui_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_chatui_desc: A comprehensive set of components for building chat interfaces with support for messages, avatars, timestamps, and interactive input.
doc_chatui_design_intent: ChatUI provides a consistent, reusable pattern for chat interface, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_chatui_f_avatar_desc: Display user avatars with images or fallback text
doc_chatui_f_input_desc: Send messages with button click or Enter key
doc_chatui_f_variants_desc: Different styles for system, admin, or bot messages
doc_chatui_interactive_desc: A fully functional chat with message sending and auto-scroll:
doc_chatui_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_chatui_msg_variants_desc: Messages support different variants for different types of content:
doc_chatui_scenario_1: Integrating ChatUI into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_chatui_scenario_2: Using ChatUI in management views for information organization.
doc_chatui_subcomponents: The ChatUI module includes several sub-components:

doc_chip_choice_self_when: When Chip functionality with standard options is required.
doc_chip_clickable_desc: When a click event is set, it acts as a button and jump-click hover effects are applied.
doc_chip_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_chip_deletable_desc: Setting onDelete displays a delete button (×).
doc_chip_design_intent: Chip provides a consistent, reusable pattern for compact element for tags or selections, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_chip_long_desc: Chips are interactive elements used for selection, filtering, or confirming input.
doc_chip_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_chip_scenario_1: Integrating Chip into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_chip_scenario_2: Using Chip in management views for information organization.
doc_chip_title: Interactive labels for selection, filtering and status
doc_controlled_component_desc: The state can be managed externally using `value` and `onChange`.
doc_custom_area: This is an area where you can write raw HTML/CSS.
doc_custom_layout_desc: Inside the Unstyled block, Storybook's default styles are not applied.
doc_datagrid_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_datagrid_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_datagrid_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_datagrid_best_practice_1: Use DataGrid consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_datagrid_best_practice_2: Test DataGrid behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_datagrid_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Table better suits the use case.
doc_datagrid_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_datagrid_choice_self_when: When DataGrid functionality with standard options is required.
doc_datagrid_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_datagrid_design_intent: DataGrid provides a consistent, reusable pattern for advanced data table with sorting, selection, and pagination, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_datagrid_infinite_scroll_desc: Use the loadMore prop to fetch additional rows as the user scrolls down. An alternative to pagination for large, continuously growing datasets such as logs or activity feeds.
doc_datagrid_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_datagrid_right_fixed_col_desc: Set fixed="right" on a column to pin it to the right edge of the grid. Useful for action columns that must remain visible during horizontal scrolling.
doc_datagrid_scenario_1: Admin Dashboard: Display user lists, order management, and product catalogs combining sorting, row selection, and pagination for efficient data browsing.
doc_datagrid_scenario_2: Report & Analytics: Browse large datasets with sticky headers and fixed columns. Combine with CSV export for offline analysis.
doc_datagrid_scenario_3: Infinite Scroll Feed: Use loadMore to append rows on scroll, providing a seamless experience for large log tables or activity feeds without page breaks.
doc_descriptionlist_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_descriptionlist_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_descriptionlist_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_descriptionlist_best_practice_1: Use DescriptionList consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_descriptionlist_best_practice_2: Test DescriptionList behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_descriptionlist_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Table better suits the use case.
doc_descriptionlist_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_descriptionlist_choice_self_when: When DescriptionList functionality with standard options is required.
doc_descriptionlist_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_descriptionlist_design_intent: DescriptionList provides a consistent, reusable pattern for key-value pair display in definition list format, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_descriptionlist_layout_desc: Switch between horizontal and vertical layouts. Useful for announcement pages or profile details.
doc_descriptionlist_layout_items_desc: Organize related information into logical blocks using multiple DescriptionList components.
doc_descriptionlist_layout_responsive_de: Automatically switch layout based on screen width (e.g., horizontal on desktop, vertical on mobile).
doc_descriptionlist_layout_vertical_desc: Display terms and descriptions stacked vertically. Ideal for narrower spaces or mobile devices.
doc_descriptionlist_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Description List component.
doc_descriptionlist_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_descriptionlist_scenario_1: Integrating DescriptionList into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_descriptionlist_scenario_2: Using DescriptionList in management views for information organization.
doc_descriptionlist_short_desc: DescriptionList displays a series of terms and their descriptions, commonly used for metadata or profile details.
doc_disabled_desc: Explains the behavior of components in a disabled state.
doc_disabled_state_desc: In the disabled state, operations are restricted, and visual feedback indicates it is disabled.
doc_divider_a11y_spec: Use aria-hidden="true" for decorative dividers with no semantic meaning, and role="separator" for logical section breaks.
doc_divider_anatomy_label: Optional text placed to complement the meaning of the section.
doc_divider_anatomy_line: A solid or dashed line indicating the separation.
doc_divider_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Divider component to visually separate content blocks.
doc_divider_choice_card_when: When you want to define elements as completely independent blocks using background colors or borders.
doc_divider_choice_divider_when: When you want to logically separate groups with a line and emphasize boundaries.
doc_divider_choice_matrix_desc: Select the optimal method based on the degree of information separation.
doc_divider_choice_spacing_when: When you want a more natural and clean separation using only whitespace (margins/padding).
doc_divider_content_guidelines: When using dividers with text, keep labels short and concise (e.g., "OR", "More Details"). Do not use a period at the end of the label.
doc_divider_design_intent: Dividers are used to visually separate groups of content and clarify the structure of information. By drawing a line between elements, they distinguish less relevant items and improve readability.
doc_divider_label_position_desc: Place labels on the 'left', 'center' (default), or 'right' side of the divider.
doc_divider_long_desc: Details and usage for the Divider component.
doc_divider_motion_spec: Usually static, but smooth fade-in/fade-out effects may be applied when showing or hiding due to layout changes.
doc_divider_orientation_desc: Use orientation='vertical' to separate items horizontally within a flex container.
doc_divider_scenario_form: Forms: Separating sections like Basic Info, Contact Details, and Other Settings.
doc_divider_scenario_list: Lists: Drawing thin lines between vertical list items to improve legibility.
doc_divider_scenario_nav: Navigation: Separating actions like "Settings" or "Logout" from other menu items.
doc_divider_with_label_desc: Add a text label or icon within the divider line for clearer sectioning.
doc_feedbackCloseButton_desc: Shared close button for Alert, Modal, etc.
doc_feedbackIcon_desc: Shared status icons for Alert, Modal, etc.

doc_heading_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.

doc_heading_choice_self_when: When Heading functionality with standard options is required.
doc_heading_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_heading_design_intent: Heading provides a consistent, reusable pattern for semantic heading elements (h1-h6) with design system styling, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_heading_levels_desc: Specify the hierarchy with the level property.
doc_heading_long_desc: A component for displaying headings. Renders tags from h1 to h6 depending on the level property.
doc_heading_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.

doc_heading_scenario_2: Using Heading in management views for information organization.
doc_heading_title: Structural elements used to define the hierarchy and organization of content.
doc_heatmap_desc: Graphical representation of data where individual values are contained in a matrix as colors.
doc_heatmap_title: A visualization tool used to represent data density or intensity through color variations.
doc_highlight_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_highlight_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.

doc_highlight_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_highlight_design_intent: Highlight provides a consistent, reusable pattern for visually emphasizing specific keywords within text, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_highlight_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_highlight_scenario_1: Integrating Highlight into workflows for data collection or display.

doc_ia_clickable_desc: Click here to perform an action.
doc_ia_empty_desc: Please upload a file or create a new entry to get started.
doc_icon_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_icon_adv_1: Consistency in design as all icons can be centrally managed
doc_icon_adv_2: Encapsulating file formats and styling makes changes easier
doc_icon_adv_3: Easily reuse the same icons in various components like buttons, list items, and form fields
doc_icon_advantages: Advantages of Making Icon an Independent Component
doc_icon_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_icon_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_icon_anim_table_row_example_desc: Spinners during button submission, indicators during search execution, icons during data updates
doc_icon_anim_table_row_features_desc: Can be displayed in sizes (`small` / `medium` / `large`) that blend naturally into existing UI layouts
doc_icon_anim_table_row_usage_desc: To indicate "processing" inline within buttons, input fields, next to text, etc.
doc_icon_anim_vs_loader: Differences Between Icon Animation and Loader Component
doc_icon_anim_vs_loader_desc: Specifying LoadingIcon or SpinnerIcon in the Icon component automatically applies a 360-degree rotation animation (wim-rotate). Separately, the Loader component also has animations indicating loading. These serve different purposes, so use them appropriately.
doc_icon_best_practice_1: Use Icon consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_icon_best_practice_2: Test Icon behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_icon_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Image better suits the use case.
doc_icon_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_icon_choice_self_when: When Icon functionality with standard options is required.
doc_icon_close_vs_small_desc: Use CloseIcon for standard 'close' actions in dialogs or headers. Use CloseSmallIcon for 'delete' actions in tags, chips, or input fields where visibility in small areas is required.
doc_icon_color_desc: Apply colors using tokens like primary, success, and error, or specify custom color values.
doc_icon_comp_usage_loading: Display while waiting for content to load
doc_icon_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_icon_design_intent: Icon provides a consistent, reusable pattern for scalable vector icons with consistent sizing and coloring, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_icon_gallery_action_desc: Icons representing user operations and actions.
doc_icon_gallery_comm_desc: Icons related to notifications and messages.
doc_icon_gallery_display_desc: Icons related to display modes and devices.
doc_icon_gallery_file_desc: Icons representing file types and media.
doc_icon_gallery_filled_desc: Mainly used for "active state," "actions to emphasize," and "improving visibility at small sizes."
doc_icon_gallery_nav_desc: Icons indicating page transitions and directions.
doc_icon_gallery_outline_desc: Mainly used for "subtle expression," "navigation," and "input assistance."
doc_icon_gallery_status_desc: Icons for state display and notifications.
doc_icon_gallery_ui_desc: Icons used as general UI elements.
doc_icon_gallery_user_desc: Icons related to user management and login.
doc_icon_loader_anim_desc: Waveform animations where multiple elements (3 bars or dots, etc.) move with a time lag (`bars` / `dots` / `pulse`)
doc_icon_loader_example_desc: Initial page load, fetching list data, transitioning between content
doc_icon_loader_feat_desc: Rich in visual variations and has a strong presence as an independent loading indicator. Supports `sm` / `md` / `lg` / `xl` sizes and multiple colors
doc_icon_loader_usage_desc: To display waiting for content to load on a full page or section basis
doc_icon_long_desc: Icon component used throughout the application

doc_icon_scenario_1: Integrating Icon into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_icon_scenario_2: Using Icon in management views for information organization.

doc_indicator_colors_desc: Change colors depending on the status.
doc_indicator_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_indicator_design_intent: Indicator provides a consistent, reusable pattern for small dot or badge overlay indicating status on another element, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_indicator_inline_desc: Enable the inline property to display the dot standalone next to text.
doc_indicator_long_desc: Indicator is a component that displays a small dot to indicate status (online, unread, error, etc.) on elements like avatars, icons, or containers.
doc_indicator_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_indicator_pulse_desc: Enable the pulse property to add an attention-grabbing animation.
doc_indicator_scenario_1: Integrating Indicator into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_indicator_scenario_2: Using Indicator in management views for information organization.
doc_interactiveArea_long_desc: InteractiveArea is a styled container component providing consistent borders, backgrounds, padding, and hover/drag state interactions. It serves as the visual foundation for Dropzone and EmptyState.
doc_interactiveArea_summary: Foundational interactive area component with borders and hover states
doc_interactiveArea_usage_desc: Used internally by Dropzone and EmptyState to provide a consistent interactive zone appearance.
doc_interactiveArea_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles and keyboard support for clickable areas.
doc_interactiveArea_anatomy_content: The slot where child elements and instructions are placed.
doc_interactiveArea_anatomy_root: The main container that handles states and layout.
doc_interactiveArea_best_practice_1: Use dashed borders for areas that signify 'placeholder' or 'drop' target functionality.
doc_interactiveArea_best_practice_2: Provide clear labels (title/description) and icons to communicate the purpose of the area.
doc_interactiveArea_choice_alt_when: When you need a more specific component like `Dropzone` or `EmptyState` which uses this internally.
doc_interactiveArea_choice_matrix_desc: Use when creating custom interactive surfaces requiring consistent design system states.
doc_interactiveArea_choice_self_when: When you're building a new component and want to reuse the standard interactive border and background styles.
doc_interactiveArea_design_intent: To provide a foundational interactive surface with consistent visual states (hover, drag, focus) for components like drop zones and empty states.
doc_interactiveArea_motion_desc: Visual states like hover and dragging are subtly animated to provide intuitive feedback.
doc_interactiveArea_scenario_1: Custom Dropzone: Creating a specialized file upload area with custom internal logic.
doc_interactiveArea_scenario_2: Interactive Empty State: A placeholder for a list that becomes a 'create' button on click.
doc_label_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_label_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_label_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_label_best_practice_1: Use Label consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_label_best_practice_2: Test Label behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_label_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Heading better suits the use case.
doc_label_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_label_choice_self_when: When Label functionality with standard options is required.
doc_label_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_label_design_intent: Label provides a consistent, reusable pattern for associating accessible text labels with form controls, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_label_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_label_scenario_1: Integrating Label into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_label_scenario_2: Using Label in management views for information organization.
doc_layout_horizontal_desc: Child items are arranged side-by-side horizontally.
doc_legend_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.

doc_legend_best_practice_2: Test Legend behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.

doc_legend_choice_self_when: When Legend functionality with standard options is required.
doc_legend_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_legend_design_intent: Legend provides a consistent, reusable pattern for providing a caption for a group of form fields within a fieldset, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_legend_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_legend_scenario_1: Integrating Legend into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_legend_scenario_2: Using Legend in management views for information organization.

doc_link_scenario_1: Integrating Link into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_link_scenario_2: Using Link in management views for information organization.

doc_list_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.

doc_list_best_practice_2: Test List behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_list_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Table better suits the use case.
doc_list_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_list_choice_self_when: When List functionality with standard options is required.
doc_list_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_list_desc: Structurally organized items in bullet points or numbered formats
doc_list_design_intent: List provides a consistent, reusable pattern for ordered or unordered list display with consistent styling, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_list_long_desc: A versatile list component for organizing and displaying related items.
doc_list_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_list_scenario_1: Integrating List into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_list_scenario_2: Using List in management views for information organization.
doc_list_sizes_desc: Supports three sizes: small, medium (default), and large.
doc_list_spacing_desc: Choose item spacing from tight, normal (default), or loose.
doc_list_title: Structurally organized items in bullet points or numbered formats
doc_list_with_icons_desc: Specify iconName on a ListItem to display an icon. Placement can be controlled with iconPosition.
doc_loader_anim_style_desc: Select different animation styles using the variant property.
doc_loader_basic_desc: Visual indicator for ongoing loading or background processing states.
doc_loader_colors_desc: Colours can be matched to various system states.
doc_loader_desc: Animated indicators to show that data is loading or processing
doc_loader_long_desc: Details and usage for the Loader component.
doc_loader_sizes_desc: Choose the appropriate size based on the display location.
doc_loader_title: Animated indicators to show that data is loading or processing
doc_markdown_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_markdown_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_markdown_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_markdown_best_practice_1: Use Markdown consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_markdown_best_practice_2: Test Markdown behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_markdown_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Code better suits the use case.
doc_markdown_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_markdown_choice_self_when: When Markdown functionality with standard options is required.
doc_markdown_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_markdown_desc: A component for safely rendering text in Markdown format.
doc_markdown_design_intent: Markdown provides a consistent, reusable pattern for rendering Markdown-formatted text as styled HTML, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_markdown_feat_gfm_desc: Tables, strikethrough, checklists, etc. are available.
doc_markdown_feat_styling_desc: Default styles tailored to the WIM UI design system are applied.
doc_markdown_long_desc: The Markdown component is used to render text written in Markdown format as appropriate HTML. Ideal for blog posts, documentation, previews of user-generated content, etc.
doc_markdown_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_markdown_notes_security_desc: Uses react-markdown internally and is designed to prevent XSS attacks by default, but care is needed for complex HTML permission settings.
doc_markdown_scenario_1: Integrating Markdown into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_markdown_scenario_2: Using Markdown in management views for information organization.
doc_kanban_a11y_desc: KanbanBoard uses role="region". Cards use role="listitem" with aria-disabled for disabled state. Column titles serve as accessible labels for each drop zone.
doc_kanban_anatomy_board: The root container that provides drag-and-drop context and holds all columns in a horizontal layout.
doc_kanban_anatomy_card: A draggable item within a column. Becomes non-draggable when disabled.
doc_kanban_anatomy_column: A vertical lane with a header (title + card count badge) and a body that acts as a drop target.
doc_kanban_best_practice_1: Keep column count to 3–6 for readability. Too many columns make it hard to see the overall workflow.
doc_kanban_best_practice_2: Card data and column assignment should be managed in parent state. Use the onCardMove callback to update state and re-render.
doc_kanban_best_practice_3: Use the disabled prop on cards that should not be moved — for example, cards that are locked by another user or in a finalized state.
doc_kanban_choice_alt_list_when: When tasks do not have stage-based progression and a simple ordered list is sufficient.
doc_kanban_choice_alt_table_when: When tasks need to be displayed in a sortable tabular format with multiple columns of metadata.
doc_kanban_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_kanban_choice_self_when: When visualizing work items that progress through distinct stages (e.g. To Do → In Progress → Done) and drag-and-drop reordering is needed.
doc_kanban_content_guideline_1: Keep card content concise — a title and optionally a short description or label is ideal.
doc_kanban_content_guideline_2: Use the cardCount prop on columns to show the number of items at a glance.
doc_kanban_desc: Drag-and-drop board for visualizing workflow stages
doc_kanban_design_intent: Kanban provides a visual way to manage work items across stages using a drag-and-drop interface. State ownership remains with the parent, keeping the component flexible for any workflow.
doc_kanban_disabled_card_desc: Cards with the disabled prop cannot be dragged and are visually dimmed.
doc_kanban_long_desc: A compound component for building Kanban boards with draggable cards across customizable columns.
doc_kanban_motion_desc: Drag-over columns are highlighted with a subtle border and background change. Dragging cards fade to indicate the source position.
doc_kanban_scenario_1: Tracking feature requests or bug reports across stages: Reported → Triaged → In Progress → Resolved.
doc_kanban_scenario_2: Managing sprint tasks across columns: Backlog → In Progress → Review → Done.
doc_ganttchart_long_desc: A Gantt chart is a bar chart that illustrates a project schedule. This component displays tasks, durations, and progress in a clear, timeline-based format.
doc_ganttchart_design_intent: To provide a highly readable and interactive way to visualize project timelines, dependencies, and progress, helping users manage complex schedules efficiently.
doc_ganttchart_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on the complexity and type of chronological information.
doc_ganttchart_choice_self_when: When you need to visualize project schedules, task durations, and overlapping activities over a specific timeline.
doc_ganttchart_choice_alt_timeline_when: When you want to show a simple vertical history of events rather than task durations.
doc_ganttchart_choice_alt_table_when: When the focus is on metadata and sorting rather than visual timeline representation.
doc_ganttchart_choice_alt_kanban_when: When the goal is to manage task status and workflow stages rather than precise timing.
doc_ganttchart_anatomy_label_panel: The left panel containing task names and labels.
doc_ganttchart_anatomy_header: The top row displaying the timeline scale (weeks/months).
doc_ganttchart_anatomy_bar: The horizontal bar representing the total duration of a task.
doc_ganttchart_anatomy_progress: The inner portion of the task bar indicating its completion percentage.
doc_ganttchart_content_guideline_1: Keep task labels concise to ensure readability in the label panel.
doc_ganttchart_content_guideline_2: Ensure task dates are valid to avoid rendering errors on the timeline.
doc_ganttchart_content_guideline_3: Group related tasks or use consistent colors to distinguish between different project phases.
doc_ganttchart_motion_desc: Smooth transitions when changing views or updating data, with subtle hover states for task bars.
doc_ganttchart_visual_guideline_progress: The progress bar should have enough contrast against the background task bar. Usually, a darker shade of the primary color is used for the completed portion.
doc_ganttchart_a11y_desc: Keyboard support and ARIA roles for navigating between tasks and interacting with the timeline.
doc_ganttchart_a11y_key_tab: Move focus between the chart and other interactive elements.
doc_ganttchart_a11y_key_arrow_down: Focus the next task in the list.
doc_ganttchart_a11y_key_arrow_up: Focus the previous task in the list.
doc_ganttchart_a11y_key_enter: Select or trigger the click handler for the focused task.
doc_ganttchart_scenario_project_desc: Mapping out product development cycles and team milestones.
doc_ganttchart_scenario_release_desc: Visualizing deployment phases and QA windows for a software release.
doc_ganttchart_best_practice_1: Avoid overcrowding the timeline with too many tasks; use filtering or pagination if necessary.
doc_ganttchart_best_practice_2: Provide tooltips for task bars to show detailed start/end dates and progress.
doc_ganttchart_best_practice_3: Sync the scroll between the label panel and the timeline for a seamless experience.
doc_ganttchart_token_primary: Used for the task bar and progress indicator.
doc_ganttchart_token_border: Used for grid lines and separators.
doc_ganttchart_token_bg: The background color of the label panel and timeline.
doc_ganttchart_token_text: The color of task labels and header dates.
doc_ganttchart_token_font: Smaller font size for timeline labels to save space.
doc_ganttchart_token_duration: Controls the speed of transitions when switching views.
doc_ganttchart_responsive_desc: On mobile, the label panel may be sticky or reduced to icons to maximize timeline visibility.
doc_ganttchart_keyboard_desc: Full keyboard navigation support for accessibility compliance.
doc_ganttchart_i18n_desc: Supports localized date formats and RTL layouts.
doc_bottomsheet_choice_bottomsheet_when: For task-oriented content on mobile that doesn't require a full-page modal.
doc_bottomsheet_choice_dialog_when: When you need high focus on a specific decision or alert on all devices.
doc_bottomsheet_choice_drawer_when: For side-oriented navigation or supplementary content on larger screens.
doc_bottomsheet_anatomy_handle: A visual indicator that the sheet can be pulled up or dismissed.
doc_bottomsheet_anatomy_title: Briefly describes the context of the bottom sheet.
doc_bottomsheet_content_limit: Avoid putting too much information; keep it focused on a single task.
doc_bottomsheet_motion_slide: Slides up from the bottom of the screen with a smooth elastic easing.
doc_bottomsheet_a11y_backdrop: Clicking the backdrop automatically dismisses the sheet.
doc_bottomsheet_features_focus_desc: Focus is trapped within the sheet when it's open.
doc_areachart_long_desc: A component for displaying AreaChart. Best for visualizing magnitudes of change over time trends and distributions.
doc_areachart_design_intent: The AreaChart is intended to provide a clear and concise visual summary of complex data patterns.
doc_areachart_choice_matrix_desc: Use AreaChart when you need to emphasize cumulative totals or area under curves.
doc_areachart_choice_self_when: When visualizing magnitudes of change over time.
doc_areachart_choice_alt_when: When the data is more suitable for a simple table or basic list.
doc_areachart_anatomy_root: The main wrapper handling responsive sizing and layout.
doc_areachart_anatomy_content: The core visualization area where bars, lines, or segments are rendered.
doc_areachart_content_guideline: Ensure labels are readable and use consistent color tokens for data mapping.
doc_areachart_motion_desc: Smooth transitions occur during initial load or data updates, respecting prefers-reduced-motion.
doc_areachart_a11y_desc: Accessible through screen readers with proper ARIA roles and labels for data points.
doc_areachart_scenario_1: Integrating into dashboards for performance tracking.
doc_areachart_scenario_2: Visualizing statistical data in complex reports.
doc_areachart_best_practice_1: Use standard color palettes to ensure high contrast and accessibility.
doc_areachart_best_practice_2: Test behavior across different screen sizes to ensure data remains readable.
doc_areachart_i18n_desc: Supports localized number formatting and date strings.
doc_funnelchart_long_desc: A component for displaying FunnelChart. Best for visualizing progressive stages in a process trends and distributions.
doc_funnelchart_design_intent: The FunnelChart is intended to provide a clear and concise visual summary of complex data patterns.
doc_funnelchart_choice_matrix_desc: Use FunnelChart when you need to emphasize drop-off rates between steps.
doc_funnelchart_choice_self_when: When visualizing progressive stages in a process.
doc_funnelchart_choice_alt_when: When the data is more suitable for a simple table or basic list.

doc_funnelchart_motion_desc: Smooth transitions occur during initial load or data updates, respecting prefers-reduced-motion.
doc_funnelchart_a11y_desc: Accessible through screen readers with proper ARIA roles and labels for data points.
doc_funnelchart_scenario_1: Integrating into dashboards for performance tracking.
doc_funnelchart_scenario_2: Visualizing statistical data in complex reports.
doc_funnelchart_best_practice_1: Use standard color palettes to ensure high contrast and accessibility.
doc_funnelchart_best_practice_2: Test behavior across different screen sizes to ensure data remains readable.
doc_funnelchart_i18n_desc: Supports localized number formatting and date strings.
doc_gaugechart_long_desc: A component for displaying GaugeChart. Best for visualizing values within a percentage range trends and distributions.
doc_gaugechart_design_intent: The GaugeChart is intended to provide a clear and concise visual summary of complex data patterns.
doc_gaugechart_choice_matrix_desc: Use GaugeChart when you need to emphasize KPI progress and milestones.
doc_gaugechart_choice_self_when: When visualizing values within a percentage range.

doc_gaugechart_best_practice_2: Test behavior across different screen sizes to ensure data remains readable.
doc_gaugechart_i18n_desc: Supports localized number formatting and date strings.
doc_heatmap_long_desc: A component for displaying Heatmap. Best for visualizing data density and intensity trends and distributions.

doc_linechart_choice_matrix_desc: Use LineChart when you need to emphasize sequential patterns.

doc_kanban_token_radius: Border radius of cards and columns.
doc_kanban_token_shadow: Shadow applied to cards during drag.
doc_kanban_responsive_desc: Adapts to mobile by stacking columns or using a tab view.
doc_kanban_keyboard_desc: Cards can be moved between columns using keyboard shortcuts.
doc_kanban_i18n_desc: Supports labels for different column states like Todo, Doing, Done.
doc_interactiveArea_i18n_desc: All user-visible text within InteractiveArea (title, description) should be passed via translation keys to support multi-language applications.
doc_avatar_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_avatar_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the alt and aria-label props.
doc_badge_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_badge_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the content prop.
doc_card_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through interactive elements inside the card. Enter or Space activates focused actions.
doc_card_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the header, body, and footer children.
doc_carousel_keyboard_desc: Arrow keys navigate between slides. Tab moves focus to navigation buttons and indicators.
doc_carousel_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label and slide content props.
doc_chip_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the chip. Enter or Space activates clickable chips. Delete or Backspace removes deletable chips.
doc_chip_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label prop or children.
doc_divider_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_divider_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label prop when using labeled dividers.
doc_emptystate_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through action buttons within the empty state. Enter or Space activates them.
doc_emptystate_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the title, description, and action label props.
doc_list_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through interactive list items. Enter or Space activates them where applicable.
doc_list_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through list item content.
doc_progress_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_progress_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label and aria-label props.
doc_tag_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the tag. Delete or Backspace removes removable tags. Enter or Space activates clickable tags.
doc_tag_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label prop or children.
doc_timeline_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_timeline_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the title and content props.
doc_tooltip_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the trigger element. The tooltip appears on focus. Escape closes it.
doc_tooltip_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the content prop.
doc_avatargroup_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_avatargroup_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the name and alt props of each Avatar.
doc_calendar_keyboard_desc: Arrow keys navigate between dates. Page Up and Page Down move between months. Home and End move to the first and last day of the month. Enter or Space selects the focused date.
doc_calendar_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through locale-aware date formatting and the aria-label props.
doc_descriptionlist_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_descriptionlist_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the term and description children.
doc_heading_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_heading_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the children prop.
doc_highlight_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_highlight_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the children and highlight props.
doc_legend_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
doc_legend_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the children prop.
doc_markdown_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through interactive elements (links, buttons) within the rendered Markdown content.
doc_markdown_i18n_desc: Pass a pre-translated markdown string through the content prop.
doc_heatmap_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus.
